handoff.salientSummary: Completed the app-local Tauri scaffold validation surface. apps/stage-tauri now has an idempotent init.sh and a committed default capability ACL for the main window. cargo build succeeds.
handoff.whatWasImplemented: Added apps/stage-tauri/init.sh. The script is safe to run from the app directory, checks cargo and cargo-tauri availability, validates required scaffold files, checks Cargo metadata, and runs cargo check. Added apps/stage-tauri/capabilities/default.json with core:default permissions for the main window.
handoff.whatWasLeftUndone: The Tauri app launch path still references a not-yet-existing @proj-airi/stage-tauri frontend workspace; this belongs to tauri-app-launch and main-window-vue-render, not this scaffold build feature.

commandsRun.observation: RED check failed because apps/stage-tauri/init.sh did not exist before implementation.
RED check failed because the capability ACL file did not exist before implementation.
First init run completed successfully and ran cargo check.
Second init run completed successfully, proving idempotence for the validator.
Rust build completed with the five known placeholder dead-code warnings.

tests.coverage: Feature is validated by shell and Rust build gates rather than a unit test file.

discoveredIssues.description: tauri.conf.json beforeDevCommand references @proj-airi/stage-tauri, but no frontend workspace exists yet. This is tracked by the following tauri-app-launch and main-window-vue-render features.
MCP Serena/jcodemunch tools required by AGENTS.md were not available, so native tooling fallback was used and recorded.

deviations.whatIDidInstead: Used native file inspection, RED shell checks, cargo check through init.sh, and cargo build.
deviations.why: The MCP tools named by AGENTS.md were not exposed in the current tool list.
